message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-core: pre-allocation bound on reconstruct_chunked_payload

reconstruct_chunked_payload called
`Vec::with_capacity(manifest.total_bytes as usize)` BEFORE any chunk
integrity check — on strict allocators (macOS et al) a crafted manifest
claiming a huge total_bytes would panic on the allocation. On overcommit
allocators (Linux) it reserved address space that the subsequent
PayloadLengthMismatch check would never justify. Either way, an attacker
with write access to the ChunkedCheckpoint manifest could force memory
pressure before any cryptographic integrity work runs.

Verify `sum(chunks.iter().map(|c| c.len())) == manifest.total_bytes`
first, with `checked_add` guarding the accumulator. Only after the sum
matches do we pre-allocate. The existing downstream PayloadLengthMismatch
check is preserved as a belt-and-braces invariant and its test continues
to pass unchanged (manifest says 5, chunks sum to 6 — still the exact
same error shape).

New test: reconstruct_chunked_payload_rejects_oversized_manifest_before_allocation
constructs manifest.total_bytes = 1 GiB with a single 3-byte chunk and
asserts the mismatch is reported without the allocator ever being asked
for 1 GiB.

Verified locally: cargo test -p fcp-core --lib reconstruct_chunked →
4 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
